div.head: The Consul at Geneva (Gilbert) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 15. The League Secretariat has circulated a communication from the Italian Government dated January 16,League of Nations, Official Journal, February 1936, p. 242. continuing its allegations of Ethiopian atrocities and use of dumdum bullets.
It concludes with the statement that these are “being reported to the International Committee of the Red Cross for necessary action.”

I now learn from Red Cross officials that they consider these matters as regulated under The Hague ConventionConvention Respecting the Laws and Customs of War on Land, Foreign Relations, 1907, pt. 2, p. 1204. and to be outside the Committee’s competence. They have, nevertheless, telegraphically informed the Ethiopian Red Cross and its own representatives in Ethiopia of the receipt of the Italian Government’s communication.
